content.title: Has the Destination of Our Emotions Changed?
content.subtitle: Does the heart that cries over a goal also cry for the suffering of the Ummah?
content.body:
The stadium lights have gone out. The cheers from the galleries have fallen silent. The calculations of victory and defeat are over. But after this month of excitement and frenzy, isn't there still one calculation left to make—an account of ourselves?

When the Destination of Our Emotions Changes

Our emotions have changed in strange ways. Who deserves our laughter and who deserves our tears? Amid the overwhelming frenzy of this technological age, perhaps we are beginning to forget these simple truths.

A single goal made us scream. The defeat of our favorite team made us cry and robbed us of our sleep. Yet when we saw the suffering of oppressed Muslims somewhere in the world, the cries of hungry children, or the deaths of helpless people, how easily we simply scrolled past!

Our fingers remained busy—perhaps our hearts were even busier elsewhere.

Our Frenzy Over a Ball

How intensely we immersed ourselves in the colorful, dazzling spectacle of the World Cup over the past month!

Staying awake through the night to watch matches, hanging flags, joining processions; from shops to school fields, from tea stalls to social media—everywhere there were discussions, arguments, and excitement.

At times, this frenzy even escalated into verbal abuse, physical fights, and, in some cases, deadly violence. Yet for a Muslim, time, emotions, and life itself all have value.

The question is: How much of ourselves did we give away over this past month to an obsession that may have distracted us from our responsibilities, our ibadah, and our humanitarian duties?

The Darkness of Conscience in the Light of Our Screens

The entire world is now before our eyes. Yet, while seeing so much, we seem to be gradually losing our ability to truly feel.

We saw the lifeless body of a child beneath the rubble, and with a single swipe of a finger, we moved on to the highlights of a football match.

On one side was death. On the other, celebration. The distance between them was just a single scroll.

Technology has shown us everything. But when it comes to deciding which scene should make us stop and which pain should bring us to tears—is our conscience still making that decision?

Let Us Ask Our Hearts, Just Once

The World Cup is over. The frenzy in the stadiums has faded. The calculations of victory and defeat have come to an end. Now, at least once, let us turn inward and look at our own hearts. If a single defeat on the football field can cause us so much pain, while all the bloodshed, tears, and helplessness of the Ummah fail to shake us from within—then has something gone terribly wrong with the map of our emotions?

Are we forgetting whose Ummah we belong to?

Are we forgetting the true purpose of our lives?

Are we forgetting for whom, and for what, our hearts were truly meant to weep?

If the answer is yes, then perhaps now is the time to sit with ourselves and reflect...

footer_button.hint: Every new day is a chance to become a better Muslim than yesterday. Muslims Day is dedicated to supporting that journey. If this mission inspires you, be part of it with a special contribution on this blessed Friday night.
